markdown:
**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 15 **Price** 700 gp --- Sharp metal jaws wind tightly into the pressure plate mechanism of this snare. When triggered, the jaws clamp shut and spin, damaging limbs in the process. The snare deals 10d8 piercing damage to the first creature to enter the square; that creature must attempt a DC 33 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the end of its next turn.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage plus 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and it becomes [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage plus 4d6 persistent bleed damage, and it becomes clumsy 2 for 1 minute.
name: Rending Snare

>
[Sulfur Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1257)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Olfactory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=246" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 15

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A thick, sulfurous, irritating gas fills this golden-yellow flask. A sulfur bomb deals the listed damage. On a hit, the target takes a –1 status penalty to Perception checks and attack rolls until the end of its next turn, or becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) on a critical hit. Creatures hit with this bomb are temporarily immune to the effects of the bomb for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    pfs="Standard"

[Bitter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1258)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 18

**Price** 135 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While you wear this acrid armor, any creature that [Engulfs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14) you or [Swallows you Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35) is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34); if it spends an action retching to reduce the sickened condition, you can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) as a reaction.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bitter

    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cold Comfort](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1259)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 18

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The contraption called cold comfort is a watertight pouch filled with a handful of small, heavy, silvery pellets. When emptied into an adjacent body of water, the pellets freeze the water's surface almost instantly, creating an ice block in a 10-foot square to a depth of 1 foot. Any creatures inside this space must attempt a DC 23 Reflex save. On a failure, the creature takes 2d6 cold damage and is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 minute or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 20) or the ice is broken. The entire ice block has AC 10, Hardness 10, and 40 Hit Points, and it's immune to critical hits, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) damage, and precision damage. The ice is strong enough to support one Large creature or up to four Medium or Small creatures. Traversing the slippery ice requires a successful DC 20 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cold Comfort (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 18

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Ice created by the pellets lasts for 1 hour or until broken.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cold Comfort (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 18

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The area increases to a 20-foot square, the save DC increases to 34, the cold damage increases to 4d6 and the Escape DC increases to 31. Ice created by the pellets lasts for 3 hours or until broken. The block of ice is strong enough to support one Huge creature, up to 2 Large creatures, or up to 8 Medium or Small creatures.
name: Cold Comfort

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cursebreak Bulwark](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1260)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 18

**Price** 14,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Tower Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4) (Hardness 14, HP 56, BT 28) is composed of interlocking hexagonal wooden tiles lined with metal. The tiles are painted a verdant green, and each is marked with a faded rune that protects against curses. While you have this shield raised, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against spells that target you, increasing to a +2 circumstance bonus against [curses](/Traits.aspx?ID=38). You can unleash the energy stored in the shield to destroy a curse before it can affect you.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are targeted with a curse spell; **Requirements** The _cursebreak bulwark_ is raised; **Effect** You activate the shield's countermeasures. It casts 8th-level [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250) with a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier of +27 against the triggering curse.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cursebreak Bulwark

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Diving Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1261)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 19

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn clothing

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Diving suits are bulky, waterproofed leather outfits with copper helmets, worn by divers and underwater salvagers. The suit grants a +1 item bonus to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) checks made underwater, and the helmet features tubes for connecting [_bottled air_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=250). When connected to a diving suit, bottled air doesn't need to be held and can be used to breathe as a free action. If you wear armor over a diving suit, you become [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) until you remove the diving suit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Diving Suit

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Juxtaposition Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1262)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 19

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Juxtaposition ammunition_ quivers with anticipation, as if the projectile wishes to constantly be in motion. When an activated _juxtaposition ammunition_ hits a creature, it doesn't deal the Strike's normal damage. Instead, the target must succeed at a DC 28 Will save or be teleported to a safe, unoccupied square of your choice within 60 feet of its original position and within the weapon's first range increment. On a critical failure, the target is also [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) from the gut-wrenching sensation of sudden movement. Any relocation from the _juxtaposition ammunition_ is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Juxtaposition Ammunition

    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Malleable Mixture](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1263)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 19

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your bones, muscles, and organs become vastly softer and more pliable. You can fit into and through small or narrow spaces as though you were smaller than your normal size, and you gain an item bonus to your Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Malleable Mixture (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 19

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You can fit into and through small or narrow spaces as though you were 1 size smaller than your normal size (minimum Small). The bonus to your DCs is +2, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Malleable Mixture (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 19

**Price** 750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You can fit into and through small or narrow spaces as though you were 2 sizes smaller than your normal size (minimum Tiny). The bonus to your DCs is +3, and the duration is 10 minutes.
name: Malleable Mixture

---

Tattletail developed this tether-covered bladder to help divers in Anuli's Crater Lake recover heavy salvage or escape dangerous underwater beasts. Pulling the ripcord causes a small, pressurized air tank to instantly inflate the bladder to the size of a rowboat, forcing up to 16 Bulk of attached items or creatures to jet to the surface of the water at a rate of 60 feet per round. Pulling the ripcord, or removing or affixing something to one of the many tethers on the recovery bladder, takes an Interact action. A recovery bladder can only be used once, as the quick inflation permanently stretches the bladder, and only a few tinkerers have the skills and equipment to refill a pressurized air tank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Recovery Bladder

A _wizard's tower_ is a tiny gaming piece carved into the form of a stone tower.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (command, envision, Interact);     **Effect** The gaming piece transforms into a fully furnished stone tower 80 feet high and 30 feet in diameter. The tower is topped by a peaked wooden roof, with a wooden door on the second floor accessed by wooden stairs on the tower's exterior. A spiral staircase in the center of the tower connects its 6 floors. The bottom floor is empty, ideal for storage, and the second floor is a great hall for audiences and guests. The third and fourth floors are divided into servants quarters and guest rooms, while the top two floors feature luxurious personal chambers and an alchemy lab. You can revert the tower back to its original state by climbing to the top floor and using an action to speak a command word.

</column>
name: Wizard's Tower

<traits>

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While arachnophobes don't need to fear this contraption's spiderlike appearance, they should probably worry if it starts ticking. When you activate the spider bomb, you place it on the ground and the clockwork spider crawls 5 feet in a straight line, continuing to advance 5 feet in the same straight line at the end of each of your turns for the next 4 rounds (traveling a total of 25 feet). If it takes any damage during this time, its bulbous abdomen detonates in a raging flame, dealing 5d6 fire damage to all creatures in a 5-foot burst (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). You can also activate it a second time with a single-action command word activation, causing it to explode. The spider bomb has an AC of 10, 5 Hit Points, and +0 to all saving throws. After it detonates, the spider bomb is completely destroyed, regardless of the effects of its detonation. If it survives to the end of the 4-round duration without being detonated, the spider harmlessly loses its explosive charge and falls apart.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Clockwork Spider Bomb

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 26 **Price** 18 gp --- Small flecks of mica, sand, and ground glass are packed into a spring-loaded canister that can be rigged to a trip wire or hidden in a container. The first creature to enter the square or open the container must attempt a DC 20 Reflex save as the area is showered with bright, glittering dust that sticks to skin, fur, and clothing. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes a –1 status penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks for 1 minute or until it uses an Interact action to remove the glitter.
**Failure** The creature takes a –2 status penalty to Stealth checks for 2 minutes or until it spends a 2-action activity, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait, to remove the glitter. If the creature was [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), the glitter outlines it for 1 round, making it [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) rather than [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) to visual senses.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the penalty is –4. The glitter sticks for 5 minutes or until the creature spends a 3-action activity, which has the manipulate trait, to remove it. The glitter continues to fall around the creature until completely removed, leaving a trail behind it as it moves and revealing its rough location; the creature is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), rather than undetected, to any creature that can see the glitter whenever it would otherwise have been undetected.
name: Glittering Snare

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Portable Gaming Hall](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1275)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 26

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L when not activated
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A portable gaming hall resembles a miniature roulette wheel, except it's marked with indecipherable runes, not numbers.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (1 minute) command, envision, Interact;     **Effect** The wheel grows into a circular room 30 feet in diameter, with a door on one side. Six chairs surround a circular table, 10 feet in diameter. The underside of the table contains retractable drawers full of dice, harrow cards, game pieces, and other gambling accouterments. A gallon of ale or a similar drink with a spigot near the bottom hangs on the door, replenishing to a full gallon each day; you can also bring in your own drinks if you require more than the gaming hall provides.

 You can flip the central table over as a 3-action activity, which has the manipulate trait, to revert the gaming hall to its original form.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Portable Gaming Hall

markdown:
**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 27 **Price** 15 gp --- You coat a small firework with a thick layer of resin and tar, so it sticks firmly onto a target. When a creature enters the square, the rocket launches at the creature and potentially sticks to it. You determine the direction the rocket faces when crafting the snare. The triggering creature must attempt a DC 20 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is dealt a glancing blow by the rocket and becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** The rocket's coating of tar and resin sticks to the creature, and it can't be shaken loose. The creature becomes flat-footed for 1 round, and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 minute or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 20).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature takes a direct hit. It takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage and is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet in the direction the rocket faces.
name: Tar Rocket Snare

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Toadskin Salve](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1277)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 27

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thick, oily salve reacts with the air to exude a toxic mucus when applied to skin. Once it's applied, you can activate the salve in one of the two ways detailed below. After either reaction has been used, the remaining mucus loses its reactive properties and falls away as the effect ends. You can only have one dose applied at a time. If you don't use either reaction, after 10 minutes, the mucus flakes away and the effect ends.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;   **Trigger** You hit a creature with a melee Strike;  **Effect** You fling mucus on the creature, dealing 1d4 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;   **Trigger** You are hit with a melee attack that deals physical damage;  **Effect** The mucus dulls the blow, granting you resistance 3 to physical damage against the triggering attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

The persistent poison damage increases to 3d4, the resistance increases to 8, and the duration increases to up to 1 hour if you don't use the reaction.
name: Toadskin Salve

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wet Shock Snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1279)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Snare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=146" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 27

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A hidden copper plate conceals a bag of electric eels. When a creature steps on the plate, the eels become agitated, and the creature takes 4d8 electricity damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a critical failure, the current causes the creature to become [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). The eels are unharmed after use, but the mechanism is broken as usual for snares.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wet Shock Snare

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Amaranthine Pavise](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1280)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 30

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Tower Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4) (Hardness 9, HP 54, BT 27) was developed by followers of [Ketephys](/Deities.aspx?ID=110), the elven god of the hunt. It's distinct for having a central ridge, and for being made from only wood and hide. Ketephys's religious symbol, a hawk rising over a silver crescent, is painted on the hide stretched across the front of the shield. Each _amaranthine pavise_ is blessed by Ketephysian priests to aid in liberating Tanglebriar and defeating the nascent demon lord [Treerazer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=394). While you have the shield raised, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against the innate spells and special abilities of [demons](/Traits.aspx?ID=42). When your allies have cover from the _amaranthine pavise_, the circumstance bonus they gain from cover to Reflex saves against area effects also applies to Fortitude and Will saves against demons' area effects.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Ketephys's wrath descends upon your foes. The shield casts good [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) with a DC of 27.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Amaranthine Pavise

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Drum of Upheaval](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1281)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 30

**Price** 60,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This heavy drum is engraved along the sides with images of centaurs in fierce combat. The drum grants you a +3 item bonus on [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks you make using the drum. Additionally, it imparts the rhythms of two songs upon your mind as soon as you touch it. One is a fast marching cadence; the other is a frenetic ritual dance. Each song has a different activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** Dozens of spectral centaurs burst from the drum and stampede in a 60-foot cone. The stampede deals 7d10 force damage (DC 43 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The drum casts a DC 43 [_earthquake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=95) spell.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [centaur](/Monsters.aspx?ID=76).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Drum of Upheaval

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Private Workshop](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1282)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 30

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L when not activated
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _private workshop_ is a model building about the size of a music box that resembles a smithy, tannery, alchemy lab, or other crafting facility.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (command, envision, Interact);     **Effect** The model workshop transforms into a full-sized square workshop of the represented type. The walls are 15 feet wide and the ceiling is 10 feet high. The workshop is stocked with mundane tools and can be used to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) items appropriate to the workshop with a +1 item bonus, but you must supply any raw materials.

 You can pull on a cord hanging from the workshop's ceiling as an Interact action to revert the _private workshop_ to its model form.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Private Workshop

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rampart Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1283)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 30

**Price** 1,900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Tower Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This massive [tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4) (Hardness 10, HP 60, BT 30) is painted a cool cyan green and is decorated with an image of Absalom's mother-sphinx emerging from a cresting wave.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Command;     **Effect** You transform the shield into a 1-foot-thick, 10-foot-by-10-foot stone rampart topped with a battlement and adorned with the flags of Absalom. If any part of the rampart would pass through any creatures or objects, the activation fails. At the center of each 5-foot length of the rampart is an arrow slit. The rampart has AC 10, Hardness 15, and 30 Hit Points. The rampart is immune to critical hits and precision damage. Attacks that would destroy the rampart cause it to instead revert back to its shield form and drop to the ground in an open space below the rampart. When this happens, the shield loses 30 Hit Points. You can Dismiss the activation, causing the wall to revert back to its shield form. The shield then returns secured to your arm if you are adjacent to the rampart, or drops to the ground in an open space if you're not adjacent to the rampart. If neither destroyed nor Dismissed, the rampart reverts back to a shield automatically after 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rampart Shield

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rhino Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1284)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 30

**Price** 22 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This arrow is made of polished animal horn, with the head of a rhinoceros carved in place of a traditional arrowhead. When an activated _rhino shot_ is fired, the ethereal form of a large rhinoceros takes shape around the projectile. On a successful Strike, the attack deals an additional 2d6 force damage, and the target must succeed at a DC 19 Fortitude save or be knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). If the hit with the _rhino shot_ was a critical success, the target is knocked prone unless it critically succeeds at its save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rhino Shot

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 30 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This white marble staff is carved into the figure of an abstract knight, its shield bearing the symbol of Lastwall, with a wickedly pointed sword made of dark wood raised high above its head. When you wield it as a weapon, it gains the [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) trait and functions as a wooden stake, allowing you to use it to stake vampires, and your Strikes with the staff gain a +1 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against undead. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Staff of Final Rest **Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 30 **Price** 240 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_disrupt undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=79)
  • **1st** [_disrupting weapons_](/Spells.aspx?ID=80), [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)
  • **2nd** _heal_, [_gentle repose_](/Spells.aspx?ID=131)
Staff of Final Rest (Greater) **Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 30 **Price** 900 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The circumstance bonus to damage rolls is +2.
  • **3rd** [_bind undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=22), _disrupting weapons_, _heal_
  • **4th** _heal_, [_holy cascade_](/Spells.aspx?ID=151)
Staff of Final Rest (Major) **Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 30 **Price** 9,000 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The circumstance bonus to damage rolls is +3.
  • **5th** [_death ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=64), _disrupting weapons_, _heal_
  • **6th** [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=114), _heal_, _holy cascade_
  • **7th** _heal_, _holy cascade_, [_sunburst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=326)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Transposition Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1286)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 31

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Transposition ammunition_ has a milky-white cast and will sometimes shift position subtly of its own accord. When you activate the ammunition, instead of making your Strike against a foe, you fire _transposition ammunition_ at any unoccupied square you can see within your weapon's first range increment and succeed without making an attack roll. You pull yourself through the [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), teleporting along with any items you are holding into the square where you shot the ammunition. If this would carry along any other creature (even one in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) space), the activation fails.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Transposition Ammunition
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 31

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Transposition Ammunition (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 31

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you teleport, you can take along up to two willing adjacent creatures. These creatures must each arrive in unoccupied squares adjacent to the _transposition ammunition_; if there's not enough space for both of them, you choose which one is teleported.
name: Transposition Ammunition

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Vampiric Scythe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1287)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Negative" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=118" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 31

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The blade of this _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) [scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39)_ is sharp enough to produce a whistling sound when swung through the air. The shaft is made of ebony wood with a sickly shine to it, much like the shine of infected wounds and contaminated water.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;   **Trigger** An enemy critically succeeds at an attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) you;  **Effect** As the scythe leaves your hand, it drinks the triggering creature's blood. The creature must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or become [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (drained 3 on a critical failure). If the triggering creature becomes drained, you gain temporary Hit Points equal to the triggering creature's level. You lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You critically succeed at a Strike against a living opponent;  **Effect** The scythe drains life essence from your opponent into you. You gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to half the slashing damage the scythe dealt to the foe. You lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vampiric Scythe

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Arctic Vigor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1288)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattoo

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo takes the shape of the face of a roaring polar bear with piercing, ice-blue eyes. You don't take damage from [extreme cold or severe cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call forth a blast of polar wind in a 10-foot burst within a range of 30 feet that deals 7d6 cold damage. All creatures in the area must attempt a DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Arctic Vigor
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Arctic Vigor (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The damage of your polar wind increases to 12d6, the range increases to 60 feet, and you can choose to increase the radius to 15 or 20 feet. While exposed to incredible cold, you only take minor cold damage every hour, instead of moderate cold damage every minute. You ignore the uneven ground and difficult terrain caused by ice and the difficult terrain caused by snow (reducing greater difficult terrain from ice or snow to normal difficult terrain).
name: Arctic Vigor

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crown of Insight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1289)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattoo

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A series of evenly spaced, unblinking tattooed eyes line your skin. When you enter an area that contains creatures within 60 feet that are unnoticed to you, the GM rolls a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) Perception check for you against the creatures' [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) DCs. On a success, the creature becomes [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) by you, rather than [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41), and on a critical success, the creature becomes [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) to you. All creatures in the area are then temporarily immune to your _crown of insight_ for 24 hours. The _crown of insight_ doesn't help you find hidden items or traps, nor can it help you discern a creature hiding in plain sight, such as a [gargoyle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=210) pretending to be an inanimate statue. Additionally, since the effect occurs when you move to enter an area that contains creatures, it doesn't detect an unnoticed creature [Sneaking](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) up on you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crown of Insight

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Enveloping Light](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1290)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattoo

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo is a series of six concentric circles that show up as a soft yellow on any skin tone. The marks carry in them a protective force that bolsters your body and soul. The first time each day that someone attempts to [Treat your Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57) and rolls a critical failure, they get a failure instead.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 5 rounds, your entire body begins to glow, with the effects of a 1st-level [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) spell. At the end of each of your turns during this time, you regain 1d4 Hit Points.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Enveloping Light
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Enveloping Light (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Any time someone rolls a critical failure to Treat your Wounds, they get a failure instead. The glow has the effects of a 4th-level _light_ spell, and you regain 2d8 Hit Points per round.
name: Enveloping Light

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reflexive Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1291)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattoo

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This subtle pattern of complex dots, sunbursts, and lines is difficult to make out, as it's a near match for the wearer's skin tone. Serving as a barrier facing inward, this tattoo prevents ostentatious expressions of spells that are internal to you, making them easier to hide.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell of 2nd level or lower that affects or targets only you, you can hide that you're casting it. This has the same effect as the [Conceal Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=640) feat.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Reflexive Tattoo

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 19"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kraken's Guard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1292)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 16, HP 96, BT 48) is painted a mottled crimson, and it always glistens as if wet. Its crest is composed of eight appendages that curl around each other, resembling the swirling tentacles of a giant [kraken](/Monsters.aspx?ID=275). Two ferocious eyes sit in its center, gleaming with an inner light.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Raise a Shield;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You unleash the kraken's wrath. You Raise the Shield. The shield's appendages briefly extend to impossible length as they wriggle and lash out at your foes, dealing 10d10 bludgeoning damage to all enemies in a 30-foot cone (DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The shield remains animated for 1 minute, during which you can use it to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) a creature within 10 feet of you without needing a free hand. When you use the shield to Grapple, you gain a +3 item bonus to your [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kraken's Guard

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Queasy Lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1293)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bull's-eye lantern is wrapped in decrepit leather skin. It constantly emits light in a 60-foot cone (and dim light in the next 60 feet). You can close or open internal shutters with an Interact action to block or reveal the light.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You slide in a magical lens that causes the lantern to emit a pale green light and then aim the lantern. All creatures in the lantern's 60-foot cone of bright light (but not those in the dim light), must attempt a DC 23 Fortitude save. On a failure, a creature becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The light then reverts to normal as the lens slides out of place.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Bulk** 1

</column>

---

The DC is 29. Creatures become sickened 2 on a failure or sickened 3 on a critical failure.

right="Item 17"

>
Queasy Lantern (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 13,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The DC is 37. Creatures become sickened 3 on a failure or sickened 4 on a critical failure.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Stone Circle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1295)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk**  when not activated
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _stone circle_ appears to be a thumb-sized rectangular gray stone until activated.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (1 minute) envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** When the circle is stood on the ground and activated, the stone grows into a massive stone pillar 13 feet high, 7 feet across, and weighing 25 tons. Identical pillars rise up from the ground marking a circle 100 feet across, with capstones connecting the pillars. There must be enough space to deploy the circle or it won't activate. During the activation, you align the _stone circle_ to face a single astronomical feature, such as the sun, the moon, or a constellation. You can revert the _stone circle_ back to its original state by using an Interact action to push over the original stone pillar. Once you do, the rest of the _stone circle_ collapses in a dramatic fashion, the stones falling, cracking, and disintegrating into dust. If you don't begin a ritual inside of the _stone circle_ within 1 day of its activation, it reverts back to its original state.

 When you cast a ritual of 5th level or lower within the _stone circle_, the first failure on a secondary check is improved by one degree of success (a critical failure becomes a failure and a failure becomes a success). Once you complete a ritual within the _stone circle_, regardless of the result, the stones collapse and it reverts back to its original form.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Stone Circle
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Stone Circle (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 38

**Price** 32,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The circle can be activated once per day. It provides its benefit when casting a ritual of any level, and it improves the secondary check with the worst degree of success by one degree of success, even if that secondary check succeeded.
name: Stone Circle

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dinosaur Boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1299)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 42

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The tough, scaled leather of these heavy boots comes from a mighty dinosaur, granting you the steadiness of a lumbering beast. Any time an action or effect would cause you to make a [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451), roll a DC 17 flat check. On a success, the forced movement fails to affect you.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=113), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You grow, gaining the effects of [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=102) for 1 minute. During that time, you gain dinosaur features; your legs and feet transform into a dinosaur's. You gain a foot unarmed attack that has the same statistics as your fist unarmed attack, except its damage die is increased from 1d4 to 1d6. Once during the duration, you can use the [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39) 3-action activity to Trample creatures one size smaller than you or smaller. This deals an amount of damage to each creature equal to that of your foot unarmed attack (including any extra weapon damage dice, bonuses, or additional damage as normal), with a DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. You can Dismiss the activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dinosaur Boots
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 42

**Price** 950 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dinosaur Boots (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 42

**Price** 9,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The activation makes you grow to size Huge, if you choose, with the effects of 4th-level _enlarge_. Either way, the damage die of your foot unarmed attack increases from 1d4 to 1d8, and you can use Trample as many times as you want during the activation.
name: Dinosaur Boots

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pillow Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1300)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 42

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The shield's blue enameled face is cool to the touch, and displays the moon's current phase at night. When you lay your head on the reverse side of this [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 6, HP 36, BT 18), it becomes as pliant and supportive as the best pillows. If you complete a period of rest using the _pillow shield_, you can choose to transfer your recovery to the shield. Instead of [recovering a number of Hit Points after resting](/Rules.aspx?ID=472), the shield is restored an equal number of Hit Points instead.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The shield creates an area of vigilance. It casts [_alarm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=7) centered on itself; you decide whether it creates a mental alarm or an audible alarm. The alarm goes off at the end of the spell's duration or when triggered as normal, whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pillow Shield

    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Psychic Brigandine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1301)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 42

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Splint Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=11)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Transparent crystals as hard as steel take the place of metal plates on this suit of _+2 [greater invisibility](/Equipment.aspx?ID=287) [splint mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=11)_. Also known as a coat of a thousand thoughts in Vudra and Jalmeray, where it was first developed by psychic warriors, it makes your mind an indomitable fortress. You gain resistance 5 to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) damage.

 Whenever you use the armor to become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), you also become psychically invisible. Each time you would be affected by an effect with the mental trait while you are invisible, attempt a DC 17 flat check. On a success, it doesn't affect you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Psychic Brigandine

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silhouette Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1306)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 43

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

In bright light, this dark cloak shimmers with patches of color that shift and move even when the cloth is still. In dim light or darkness, the cloak seems to blend into your surroundings and grants you a +3 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Interact (1 minute);     **Effect** You and objects you wear or carry fall into your shadow, becoming a two-dimensional silhouette. In this form you aren't incorporeal, but you also don't have a solid form and don't occupy your space, allowing other creatures to pass through or end their turn there. You gain resistance 20 to all damage except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) damage, damage from effects with the [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) trait, and damage from Strikes with the [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune. This increases to resistance 40 against nonmagical damage. Other creatures can't attempt Strength-based skill checks against you. Your land and climb Speeds both become 40 feet. You can't use actions except [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), Step, or Stride actions using the granted Speeds or an Interact activation to return to your normal form. If your current space doesn't have sufficient room for you to return to your normal form, you return to your normal form in the nearest open space.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You take damage;  **Effect** You fold the cloak over yourself and vanish into your shadow for a moment, gaining the benefits of the first activation until the end of the current creature's turn. The damage resistance applies to the triggering damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silhouette Cloak

    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Singularity Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1307)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 43

**Price** 550 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Singularity ammunition_ seems to pull in the light around it, swirling along the surface in a misty pattern. An activated _singularity arrow_ creates a strong gravitational force, centered on the creature hit. All creatures in a 10-foot emanation from the target must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or be [pulled](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 5 feet closer to the target. The singularity then explodes, dealing 7d12 bludgeoning damage to the original target and all creatures in a 5-foot emanation (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Singularity Ammunition

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 43 **Price** 6 gp --- You hide insulating crystals or other material that releases a strong static charge when the first creature enters the snare's square. That creature must attempt a DC 18 Reflex saving throw. **Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target takes 1 [persistent electricity damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and takes a –1 circumstance penalty to saving throws against electricity effects for as long as the persistent electricity damage lasts.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the target attracts nearby dirt and dust. If the target is or becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), it's merely [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) to creatures that have sight as a precise sense. This effect lasts as long as the persistent electricity damage does.
name: Static Snare

    pfs="Standard"

[Brewer's Regret](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1309)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When a brewer makes a batch of something they'd rather not drink, they often boil it down; add myrrh, mugwort extract, and violet salt; and sell it to chefs looking for cheap sandwich fillings. The thick, salty sourness ruins the taste of most other food, but it also creates a strong desire to live to taste anything else. For 1 hour after consumption, you gain a +2 item bonus to saves against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) and [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) effects. In addition, your [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) value decreases by 1 (minimum 0). You can reduce the doomed condition with brewer's regret only once per day, and after you do, you can't reduce the doomed condition from the [_restoration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=258) spell that same day (or vice versa).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

You can reduce the doomed condition with brewer's regret only once per day, and after you do, you can't reduce the doomed condition from the [_restoration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=258) spell that same day (or vice versa).

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon's Blood Pudding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1310)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While some unscrupulous chefs claim that this savory pudding is made with real dragon's blood, its crimson color and acrid smell actually come from bloody mandrake paste, ginger root, and distilled terrap sap. This potent combination singes the nostrils and throat, removing effects that make you sluggish. When you consume the pudding, it attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with the listed counteract modifier to remove the [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) condition from a single source, using the source of that condition to determine the counteract level and DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dragon's Blood Pudding (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The pudding has a +6 counteract modifier.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dragon's Blood Pudding (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The pudding has a +9 counteract modifier. You also become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 round. While taking the additional action from being quickened, you scream, as a result of both the flavor and the mandrake paste's properties. You can use the additional action to Step, Stride, Strike, or [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53). If you choose to Demoralize, you gain a +2 item bonus to the [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dragon's Blood Pudding (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 425 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The pudding has a +20 counteract modifier. You become quickened as per the 5th-level version, except you gain a +3 item bonus to the Intimidation check.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dragon's Blood Pudding (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 5,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The pudding has a +31 counteract modifier. You become quickened as per the 5th-level version, except the effect lasts for 2 rounds and you gain a +4 item bonus to the Intimidation check.
name: Dragon's Blood Pudding

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fire and Iceberg](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1311)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Fiery Anulite paprika, sunrise cinnamon, and winterbite are hidden inside this salad's translucent leaves, bringing the cool of snow and the heat of steam to every bite. The dueling sensations make some diners sweat and others shiver, but all leave with a lasting sense of heat and cold that make other sources pale in comparison. When you consume the salad, you gain resistance 5 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) and [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fire and Iceberg
</title>

<traits>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Impossible Cake](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1312)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) “Type of item” refers to a broad category of items such as books, spells, etc, not a specific object._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sleight-of-hand for the taste buds is flavored with absinthe and honey and always resembles something completely unlike a cake, from a pile of armor to a bowl of soup. Eating the cake gives you the confidence to make the impossible seem possible: for 10 minutes after the meal, you gain a +2 item bonus to your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46), as well as to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) to convince others that you possess knowledge about the type of item that the cake resembles.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Impossible Cake
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Impossible Cake (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3 and the effect lasts 30 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Impossible Cake (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 46

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +4 and the effect lasts 1 hour.
name: Impossible Cake

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Server's Stew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1314)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 47

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The type of animal tongue used in this tomato-based stew shifts from region to region, but it's always cooked with spirit of wine and seasoned with [refined pesh](/Equipment.aspx?ID=626). Eating this restaurant-staff favorite empowers you to speak and understand a single language for 1 hour. This must be a language the stew's cook could speak and understand, chosen at the time of the stew's creation. It also helps your words land smoothly, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks made in that language for the same duration. It doesn't allow you to read the newly acquired language in its written form.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Server's Stew

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thrice-Fried Mudwings](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1315)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 47

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Frying mudwings—the remains of magical winged creatures found in blighted swamps—is a delicate balance. Cooked for too long and they lose their potency; cooked too briefly and the toxins in their system could end up harming whoever eats them. When you consume a perfectly cooked tapas, you grow two sets of mudwings that grant you a fly Speed of 30 feet or your Speed, whichever is lower, for 10 minutes. You can also use the following Activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;   **Trigger** You would take damage from a physical attack;  **Effect** You intercept the attack with a pair of your wings. You gain resistance 15 against physical damage for the triggering attack only, shattering one set of your wings in the process. The first time you use this Activation, your fly Speed becomes 15 feet, or half your Speed, whichever is lower, for the duration. After the second time, your mudwings are shattered entirely, ending the effect. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Thrice-Fried Mudwings

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dweomerweave Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1316)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 50

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn clothing

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This robe is made from dweomerweave, a magical fabric created by spinning minor illusions into the threads of [ether spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=203) silk. Dweomerweave is naturally hazy and translucent. It can take on the illusory appearances of other fabrics and garments, and _dweomerweave robes_ are coveted by adventurers who want to travel light without compromising on fashion.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain the effects of a 1st-level [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) except that the illusion only alters the appearance of the _dweomerweave robe_, changing it into another garment with an appearance of your choice. The spell persists until the next activation. You can Dismiss the activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dweomerweave Robe

Experimental clothing is custom tailored in radical, avant-garde styles by independent dressmakers, utilizing expensive or eccentric materials and unconventional patterns. At the GM's discretion, wearing experimental clothing may impart a +1 item bonus or –1 item penalty on checks to [Make An Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50), depending on the target's fashion sense.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mirror Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1319)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 50

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn clothing

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Thousands of small, reflective, mirrored glass shards have been carefully stitched down this long silk duster.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;    **Requirements** The _mirror robe_ was last activated to draw attention toward you, or you haven't used the _mirror robe_ today; **Effect** The _mirror robe_ [Creates a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=45) for you with a +9 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) modifier.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163))   **Requirements** The _mirror robe_ was last activated to divert attention away from you and you are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) by at least one foe; **Effect** You draw attention toward yourself. Choose one foe to which you were hidden or undetected. You reveal yourself to all, becoming [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=27). The foe you chose diverts its attention to you, becoming [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to your allies until the beginning of your next turn. If you are [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) or otherwise can't become observed, you can't use this activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mirror Robe

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pickpocket's Tailoring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1320)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 50

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** sewn into clothing

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Pickpocket's tailoring modifies an existing outfit by adding concealed interior pockets and strategically opened seams for hiding small objects inside the lining. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) of light Bulk or less in the pockets. When you get a failure (but not a critical failure, which works as normal) on a Stealth check to Conceal such an Object, observers know you're concealing an object somewhere, but they don't find the object unless they succeed at a DC 20 Perception check to locate the seams in the garment.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pickpocket's Tailoring

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Quick Wig](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1321)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 50

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _quick wig_ magically conceals your natural hair while worn, eliminating the need to braid or pin your hair beneath it. In addition, your facial hair and eyebrows change color to match the wig while you wear it. When used as part of a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) to go unrecognized by changing your hair, you no longer require a [disguise kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), you gain a +1 item bonus on the check, and you reduce the time to create the disguise from 10 minutes to 5 minutes. You still need a disguise kit and the full time if you're using cosmetics and other props to change other aspects of your disguise or Impersonating a specific person, and the wig only provides its item bonus when Impersonating a specific person if that person's hair color and style match the wig's. _Quick wigs_ detangle themselves while not in use.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Quick Wig

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wardrobe Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1323)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 51

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This large stone eye is the size of a fist and decorated with elaborate carvings of robes and other garments.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (1 minute) envision, Interact;     **Effect** You enter a brief trance in which you envision yourself entering a large dressing room. You maneuver through the endless dressing-room mindscape and search for whatever articles of clothing, accessories, and other garments you desire. After the activation, you gain the effects of [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), except that it only causes you to appear to be wearing the exact outfit you selected within the mindscape. The illusion also disguises the _wardrobe stone_ to match your outfit and not appear out of place, such as by appearing to be a glove or bracelet, though it continues to occupy your hand while you're under the effects of _illusory disguise_. You can actively Dismiss the Spell, but the effects are also dismissed immediately when you're no longer holding the _wardrobe stone_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wardrobe Stone (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 51

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wardrobe Stone (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 51

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to a specific [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill associated with your outfit, such as Carpentry Lore when wearing a carpenter's outfit or Cooking Lore when wearing a chef's outfit.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (1 minute) envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_prying eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=239).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wardrobe Stone (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 51

**Price** 55,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain +3 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight and a +3 item bonus to a specific Lore skill associated with your outfit.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (1 minute) envision, Interact;     **Effect** You cast _prying eye_.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> (10 minutes) envision, Interact;     **Effect** The wardrobe stone splits open and permanently transforms into a specific type of magical robe of your choosing. It can transform into any 20th-level or lower magical robe to which you have access, except for items that can't normally be [Crafted](/Actions.aspx?ID=43), such as [artifacts](/Traits.aspx?ID=253). This process is irreversible.
name: Wardrobe Stone
